burialPlace.address: Ludlow Old Cemetery, Ludlow, Shropshire Unitary Authority, Shropshire, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Lawrence de Lodelowe was born circa 1275 in Shropshire, England. He was the eldest son of William Ludlowe and Matilda de Hodnet.

Laurence de Lodelowe was married to a woman named Hawise.

William de Ludlowe, Lawrence's father, died in 1316. At this time, his mother was remarried to a man named de Wyn. Matilda de Hodnet de Ludlowe de Wyn died circa 1347. Matilda left just one heir, a son named Lawrence de Ludlowe.

Unfortunately, Laurence de Ludlowe got into some trouble for a violent physical assault upon a man named John Wyard. He became an outlaw to King Edward III, who seized all of his property for the crown in 1348.

Lawrence de Ludlowe died an outlaw in 1353.

As some small measure of consolation, John de Ludlowe, Matilda's grandson, was granted the position of High Sheriff of Shropshire in 1360.


Sources
↑ Wrottesley, George. Pedigrees from the plea rolls: collected from the pleadings in the various courts of law, A.D. 1200 to 1500, from the original rolls in the Public Record Office. 1905, p 53.
Treswell, Robert; Vincent, Augustine; Camden, William; Grazebrook, George; editors: The visitation of Shropshire, taken in the year 1623, by the College of Arms, Great Britain. Publication date 1889. PP 342 - 343. Ludlowe of Shropshire Pedigree.
↑ Wrottesley, George. Pedigrees from the plea rolls: collected from the pleadings in the various courts of law, A.D. 1200 to 1500, from the original rolls in the Public Record Office. 1905, p 35 & p 99.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle於1290年之前出生時，他的父親Sir William Ludlowe40歲，母親Matilda Maud Hodnet34歲。他於1323年與Hawise D'Ervassal在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1353年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾斯托可賽，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾盧德洛福。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ludlow,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from the Old English river name Hlūde (from hlūd ‘loud, roaring’) referring to the Teme river + hlāw ‘hill’. This name was taken Ireland in the 13th and 17th centuries. See also Laidlaw .

variants.name: Laidlaw
meanings: Scottish: Border surname found mainly around Selkirk and the vales of Ettrick and Yarrow; a Scottish development of   Ludlow  .

variants.name: Ledlow
meanings: English, Irish, or Scottish: variant of   Ludlow   or   Laidlaw  .

variants.name: Ludlam
meanings: English: possibly from a misreading of w as m in Ludelawe, a recorded medieval spelling of the placename Ludlow in Shropshire. Compare Ludlow .

nameMeanings.name: Laurence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a French form of Latin Laurentius ‘man from Laurentum’. Laurentum was a town in Latium, which may have got its name from Latin laurus ‘laurel’, although it is more probably of pre-Roman origin. The given name was moderately popular in the Middle Ages (when it was used for girls as well as boys), under the influence of a 3rd-century saint who was one of the seven deacons of Rome. He was martyred in 258 . The legend is that, having been required to hand over the Church's treasures to the civil authorities, he assembled the poor and sick and presented them. For this act of Christian defiance, he was roasted to death on a gridiron. In England the name is also associated with St Laurence of Canterbury ( d. 619 ). A more recent influence has been the actor Sir Laurence Olivier ( 1907–89 ). See also Lawrence .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle was born before 1290, his father, Sir William Ludlowe, was 40 and his mother, Matilda Maud Hodnet, was 34. He married Hawise D'Ervassal in 1323, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1353, in Stokesay, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle est né avant 1290, son père, Sir William Ludlowe, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Matilda Maud Hodnet, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Hawise D'Ervassal en 1323, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1353, à Stokesay, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Ludlow, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle vor 1290 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Ludlowe 40 und seine Mutter Matilda Maud Hodnet 34. Er hat 1323 in Shropshire, England Hawise D'Ervassal geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1353 in Stokesay, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle è nato il prima del 1290, il suo padre, Sir William Ludlowe, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Maud Hodnet, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Hawise D'Ervassal nel 1323, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1353, nel Stokesay, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Ludlow, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castleが1290年以前に生まれた時，父Sir William Ludloweは40歳，母Matilda Maud Hodnetは34歳であった。1323年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでHawise D'Ervassalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1353年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアストケサイで死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアルドロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle(이)가 1290년 이전에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Ludlowe(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Maud Hodnet(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1323년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Hawise D'Ervassal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1353년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 스토케지에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 루들로에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle nasceu antes 1290, seu pai, Sir William Ludlowe, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Maud Hodnet, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Hawise D'Ervassal em 1323, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1353, em Stokesay, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Ludlow, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle родился перед 1290 г., его отцу по имени Sir William Ludlowe было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Maud Hodnet, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise D'Ervassal в 1323 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1353 г. в местности стокесеи, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности лудлоу, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Laurence de Ludlow, Lord of Stokesay Castle nació antes de 1290, su papá, Sir William Ludlowe, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Matilda Maud Hodnet, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Hawise D'Ervassal en 1323, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1353, en Stokesay, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Ludlow, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
